req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 2
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The violation was test[1] having kind=negative with heuristic=BVA. BVA is reserved for boundary and positive kinds in this rule set. The fix is to change test[1] (oversized file rejection) to use heuristic=NEG, which correctly characterises a negative test that exercises an error pathway rather than a boundary value. All other tests are preserved unchanged. The five tests cover: (1) valid PDF upload positive EP, (2) oversized file negative NEG, (3) exact boundary positive BVA, (4) unsupported MIME negative DT, and (5) idempotent retry positive STC.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_returns_201_and_queues_virus_scan
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A well-formed PDF file of 1 MB is available
actions: Authenticate and obtain session token via <auth_fixture>
Generate a 1 MB synthetic PDF via <make_file_fixture>
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the PDF file
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty document_id
trace: R-WEB-078: valid PDF within size limit must be stored
R-WEB-078: virus scan must be queued on successful upload
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_valid_pdf_returns_201_and_queues_virus_scan(
    auth_headers, make_upload_file, api_client
):
    file_bytes = make_upload_file(size_bytes=1 * 1024 * 1024, mime="application/pdf")
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")}

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files=files,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("document_id"), "document_id must be present and non-empty"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued", (
        f"virus_scan_status must be 'queued', got {body.get('virus_scan_status')}"
    )

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A PDF file of exactly 10 MB + 1 byte is available
actions: Authenticate and obtain session token via <auth_fixture>
Generate a synthetic PDF of 10 485 761 bytes (10MB + 1B) via <make_file_fixture>
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the oversized file
Capture HTTP response status
expected: HTTP 413 Request Entity Too Large is returned
No document_id is present in the response
trace: R-WEB-078: files > 10 MB must be rejected with HTTP 413
tests.executable:
import pytest

ONE_BYTE_OVER_LIMIT = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1

def test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413(
    auth_headers, make_upload_file, api_client
):
    file_bytes = make_upload_file(
        size_bytes=ONE_BYTE_OVER_LIMIT, mime="application/pdf"
    )
    files = {"file": ("big.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")}

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files=files,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" not in body, "document_id must not be present on rejection"

tests.name: test_upload_file_at_exact_10mb_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A PDF file of exactly 10 485 760 bytes (10 MB) is available
actions: Authenticate and obtain session token via <auth_fixture>
Generate a synthetic PDF of exactly 10 485 760 bytes via <make_file_fixture>
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the boundary-sized file
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty document_id
No 413 error is raised at the exact limit
trace: R-WEB-078: files of exactly 10 MB must be accepted (upper inclusive boundary)

tests.name: test_upload_unsupported_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A file with MIME type text/plain (.txt) is available
actions: Authenticate and obtain session token via <auth_fixture>
Generate a 1 KB synthetic text file via <make_file_fixture>
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with text/plain MIME type
Capture HTTP response status
expected: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type is returned
No document_id is present in the response
trace: R-WEB-078: only PDF, PNG, JPG are in the allow-list; all others must return 415

tests.name: test_upload_idempotent_on_retry_returns_same_document_id
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A valid PNG file of 500 KB is available
A unique idempotency key is generated for the request
actions: Authenticate and obtain session token via <auth_fixture>
Generate a 500 KB synthetic PNG via <make_file_fixture>
Generate a UUID as the Idempotency-Key header value
POST multipart/form-data with the PNG and the Idempotency-Key header (first attempt)
Record the returned document_id
Repeat the identical POST (retry, same Idempotency-Key) (second attempt)
Compare document_ids from both responses
expected: document_id from first response equals document_id from second response
Exactly one virus scan job is queued (not duplicated)
trace: R-WEB-078: the upload action must be idempotent under retry
